problem:
The first six rows of Pascal's triangle are shown below, beginning with row zero. Except for the $1$ at each end, row $4$ consists of only even numbers, as does row $2.$ How many of the first $20$ rows have this property? (Don't include row $0$ or row $1$). \begin{tabular}{ccccccccccc}
&&&&&1&&&&&\\
&&&&1&&1&&&&\\
&&&1&&2&&1&&&\\
&&1&&3&&3&&1&&\\
&1&&4&&6&&4&&1&\\
1&&5&&10&&10&&5&&1\\
\end{tabular}

classified_questions.question: What is Pascal's triangle and how is it constructed?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we determine whether an entry in Pascal's triangle is even or odd without computing the entry itself?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the pattern in the parity of entries in Pascal's triangle and how can we describe it?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we use the parity pattern to determine the number of rows in Pascal's triangle that consist only of even numbers (excluding the first two rows)?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are there other interesting patterns or properties of Pascal's triangle that can be explored using similar methods?
classified_questions.label: r
